reason: Dr. Strangelove’s savage wit and dead-on satire tear apart Cold War absurdity with gleeful venom.
His Girl Friday’s lightning repartee skewers newsroom chaos with razor-sharp, hilarious cynicism.
Life of Brian’s uproarious assaults on dogma and crowd-pleasing satire blister orthodoxies.
MASH’s gallows humor and sly barbs at military folly deliver smart, irreverent scorn.
Fish Called Wanda’s gleeful betrayals and smart-aleck one-liners mercilessly mock human greed.
Bullets Over Broadway’s backstage carnage ably skewers artistic egos with wickedly clever jabs.
Get Shorty’s noirish Hollywood send-up trades punchy dialogue for slick cultural satire.
Monty Python and the Holy Grail’s anarchic medieval send-up skewers legend with gleeful absurdity.
A Clockwork Orange’s chilling surrealism and social critique carve anarchic wit into a brutal fable.
Network’s manic media meltdown? Sorry–not here. So we revisit MASH’s scathing carnivals of military irony.
